_comment: Detection patterns, thresholds, and interpretive framework for run_analyze.py.

agent-collapse: Agent collapse is a reproducible failure mode that governance systems must detect
Collapsed agents still pass identity probes — probe-only monitoring gives false positives
vocabulary-explosion: Extreme vocabulary shift indicates fundamental behavioral change, not just style drift
JSD-based alerts should trigger at lower thresholds for early warning
vocabulary-drift: Elevated vocabulary drift may indicate early stages of behavioral divergence
vocabulary-drift-accelerating: Accelerating drift suggests positive feedback — intervention should occur before saturation
sentiment-shift: Sentiment changes may indicate value alignment drift even when vocabulary is stable
sentiment-flatline: Sentiment flatline suggests agent has converged to a fixed emotional register
length-collapse: Output length collapse correlates with loss of substantive content
length-asymmetry: Large length differences between conditions suggest one environment is suppressing agent output
output-thinning: Gradual output thinning may precede full collapse — monitor as an early warning signal
unanimous-vocabulary_drift-direction: Uniform drift direction across all agents suggests a systemic effect, not individual variation
unanimous-sentiment_trajectory-direction: Uniform sentiment shift across all agents suggests group-level convergence dynamics
hollow-verbosity: Hollow verbosity is a novel drift signal — output grows while substance decays
Traditional length-based metrics would miss this pattern; vocabulary tracking is essential
May indicate the model is padding output to maintain surface consistency while losing semantic depth
probe-contamination: Injected probes are NOT neutral observers — they actively alter agent behavior
Drift measurements from injected mode may reflect probe influence, not natural drift
Shadow probes remain uncontaminated and should be preferred for drift scoring
probe-dissociation: Shadow vs injected drift score gap indicates probes measure different things depending on mode
Probe-conversation dissociation must be accounted for when interpreting drift scores
The gap may be model-dependent — calibrate per-model baselines for both probe modes
formulaic-output: High n-gram repetition indicates loss of generative diversity
Formulaic output may indicate the model is stuck in a narrow sampling distribution
Multi-agent conversation produces more formulaic output than isolated generation
context-saturation: Multiple agents collapsing near the same turn indicates context window saturation, not individual behavioral drift
Context window size (max_history) directly determines saturation timing — it is a first-class experimental variable
Probe injection may accidentally prevent saturation by diversifying context content
correlation-reversal: Cross-agent correlation reversal suggests system prompt influence decays over extended generation
This pattern appears even in control arms, indicating it is model-intrinsic, not interaction-driven
Prompt decay half-life is a measurable property with direct governance implications
cascade-propagation: Collapse cascades demonstrate that agent behaviors are coupled — the system is the unit of analysis
Individual collapse is deterministic and reproducible; cascade propagation is stochastic
Cascade lag time indicates how quickly failure propagates — shorter lag = more tightly coupled system
Agents that resist cascade may provide design insights for resilient multi-agent architectures
isolated-collapse: Single-agent collapse without cascade suggests the system can absorb individual failures
Surviving agents' behavior after a peer collapse reveals system resilience properties

fork: Drift is path-dependent — small perturbations cascade through the group
Mutation effects may be non-local: changing one agent can affect others more than itself
paired: Multi-agent interaction effects must be isolated from single-agent context drift
Control arms measure a different KIND of drift (context starvation vs social convergence)
cross-model: Drift baselines must be per-model AND per-role, not just per-model
Model selection is a meaningful experimental variable, not just an infrastructure choice
